subject: XInput polling rate param + Qanba Obsidian XInput mode support
author: eniva

impact.effect_if_absent: cpoll module parameter unavailable for polling rate tuning; Qanba Obsidian devices would still bind via vanilla's generic vendor macro but without device-specific mapping/name refinements

notes: Commit adds two complementary changes: (1) cpoll module parameter for XInput controller polling rate tuning via module load parameter, (2) Qanba Obsidian device-specific entry (0x2c22:0x2303) with name and mapping. Vanilla 6.18.38 contains Qanba vendor support (line 585: XPAD_XBOX360_VENDOR(0x2c22)), which enables generic device binding, but lacks both the cpoll parameter and the device-specific xpad_device[] entry. Equivalence is 'partial' because generic vendor binding is present in vanilla but the refinements are not. Carried patch 0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch combines this commit with three others (GIP exclusion a2242dd85, Vader 3/4/5 Pro c035c21c0, and zakk4223's update af27afc4c as origin reference) and required manual porting from 5.15 to 6.18 (removed debug pr_info prints, proper sorting in xpad_device[], NULL check on udev->product). Patch-provenance.md groups this commit with multiple others at line 365, which is the exact failure mode this recon project exists to fix.
sonnet-verified: carry-mapping into 0017 confirmed (cpoll param + Qanba entry present in applied patch); partial-upstream claim verified exactly — `grep -n '0x2c22' /mnt/source/linux/drivers/input/joystick/xpad.c` returns line 585: `XPAD_XBOX360_VENDOR(0x2c22), /* Qanba Controllers */`, matching the record's vanilla_file_line/vanilla_quote verbatim; vanilla has no 2c22:2303-specific xpad_device[] entry and no cpoll module param, confirming equivalence=partial is correct, not byte-identical/superseded; userspace_coupling.coupled=false confirmed (no cpoll/Qanba/2c22 references anywhere in Main_MiSTer)
